findings.title: Output signal 'out' is not pinned by any constraint
findings.description: 'out' is declared as a circuit output but does not appear on the LHS of '<==', '===', or '$$' anywhere in the source. A prover can choose any value for 'out' and the verifier will accept it. A bare '<-- out = <expr>;' is only a witness assignment, not a constraint.
findings.fix: Add a constraint that pins 'out', e.g. 'out <== <expr>;' or 'out === <expr>;' on the line below the declaration.

findings.title: Witness assignment '<-- out' has no matching constraint
findings.description: Circom 2.x '<--' is a witness-only assignment and adds NO constraint. The value 'a * b' is therefore prover-chosen unless a matching '<==', '===' or '$$' appears on the next non-empty, non-comment line.
findings.fix: Replace 'out <-- a * b;' with 'out <== a * b;', or add a separate 'out === a * b;' constraint directly under the assignment.

findings.title: Witness assignment '<-- sum' has no matching constraint
findings.description: Circom 2.x '<--' is a witness-only assignment and adds NO constraint. The value 'a + b' is therefore prover-chosen unless a matching '<==', '===' or '$$' appears on the next non-empty, non-comment line.
findings.fix: Replace 'out <-- a * b;' with 'out <== a * b;', or add a separate 'sum === a + b;' constraint directly under the assignment.

findings.title: Public input 'pub' is not range-checked
findings.description: 'pub' is a public input used in arithmetic. Without a range check (Num2Bits, RangeCheck, or bit-decomposition), a malicious prover can submit a value outside the intended bit-width and exploit field-modulus wraparound.
findings.fix: Add a Num2Bits(n) / range-check sub-template that bounds 'pub' to its intended bit-width.

findings.title: Public input 'a' is not range-checked
findings.description: 'a' is a public input used in arithmetic. Without a range check (Num2Bits, RangeCheck, or bit-decomposition), a malicious prover can submit a value outside the intended bit-width and exploit field-modulus wraparound.
findings.fix: Add a Num2Bits(n) / range-check sub-template that bounds 'a' to its intended bit-width.

findings.title: Public input 'b' is not range-checked
findings.description: 'b' is a public input used in arithmetic. Without a range check (Num2Bits, RangeCheck, or bit-decomposition), a malicious prover can submit a value outside the intended bit-width and exploit field-modulus wraparound.
findings.fix: Add a Num2Bits(n) / range-check sub-template that bounds 'b' to its intended bit-width.

explain_circuit.summary: Offline scan of a Circom circuit. The summary is rule-based: public and private signals are listed from declarations, and the signal->constraint map is best-effort. A live model call would produce a richer explanation.
reviewerNotes: Run the audit_circuit tool with a real CYSIC_API_KEY for a deeper explanation.
